news.headline: Memecoin Infrastructure is Taking Over DeFi
news.summary:
- Memecoin-specific applications generated significant revenue in DeFi in June, with Pump.Fun and Telegram bots accounting for nearly $50 million.\
- Pump.Fun was the second-highest grossing product in DeFi, surpassing Ethereum in 24-hour revenue on July 1.\
- Telegram trading bots like Trojan, BananaGun, Maestro, and BonkBot also made the top 15 revenue-generating products, collectively bringing in $27.6 million in the last 30 days.\
- The rise of memecoins has led to a competitive field for onchain trading services, with developers utilizing tools like Telegram and Discord bots, wallet trackers, and social media trackers.\
- Unibot, a popular Telegram trading bot, experienced a significant decline in token value and daily volume due to competition from BananaGun and the popularity of Solana memecoins.\
- BananaGun, on the other hand, has maintained a high token value and trading volume, integrating with other chains and trading an average of $19 million per day.

First Mover Americas: Bitcoin Layer 2s Have Their Moment
news.summary:
- Bitcoin layer 2 tokens, such as STX, ELA, and SAVM, have outperformed Bitcoin since the recent mining reward halving.\
- Grayscale plans to launch a spinoff version of its Bitcoin ETF with a lower fee of 0.15%.\
- Woo X becomes the first exchange to offer retail customers exposure to tokenized U.S. Treasury bills through its RWA Earn Vaults product.\
- The premium for cash and carry arbitrage strategy has declined sharply, reducing its appeal.

news.headline: Pump.fun Is Back on Top and Solana Meme Coins Are Soaring: Here’s Why
Funding Wrap: Gaming continues to draw investor intrigue
news.summary:
- Web3 gaming startups are attracting investment, with several companies announcing funding rounds.\
- Gaming studio Carbonated raised $11 million for its NFT-enabled mobile shooter game MadWorld, with participation from Com2uS and a16z.\
- MetaCene, the developers of a massively multiplayer online game, secured $10 million in a Series A round, with participation from The Spartan Group and Animoca Ventures.\
- Blockus raised $4 million in pre-seed funding for its service helping game developers add blockchain-enabled features.\
- Modular layer-2 blockchain Eclipse raised $50 million in a Series A round led by Placeholder Ventures and Hack VC.\
- Other notable fundraises include Polyhedra raising $20 million, D2X securing $10 million, Clique announcing an $8 million Series A, and UXLink raising $9 million.\
- Ethereum's fee-reducing Dencun upgrade did not impact Eclipse's use of high-throughput network Celestia for transaction data posting.\
- Eclipse is expected to go to mainnet next quarter.

news.headline: Runes Protocol Launches on Bitcoin, Sending Fees Soaring as Users Rush to Mint Tokens
news.summary:
- Runes Protocol, created by Bitcoin builder Casey Rodamor, has launched on Bitcoin.\
- Runes allows users to "etch" and mint tokens on-chain, similar to meme coins.\
- The first Runes project to mint was Rodamor's UNCOMMON•GOODS project.\
- Runes minters have already paid 78.6 BTC in fees (~$4.95 million) to buy tokens.\
- The quality of the ticker and being an early project are factors in determining a Runes project's viability.\
- The long-term value of Runes projects is uncertain, and the pre-mined tokens may have an advantage.

Binance Will Start Converting Monero to USDC on September 2 as Part of Delisting
news.summary:
- Binance will convert Monero (XMR) and 14 other tokens to USD Coin (USDC) starting September 2, 2024, as part of its delisting process.\
- The conversion rate will be based on the average exchange rate from September 2, 2024, to March 1, 2025, with USDC available in users' wallets by March 1, 2025.\
- Affected tokens include Bitcoin Gold (BTG), Bitcoin Standard Hashrate Token (BTCST), Bitshares (BTS), District0x (DNT), Groestlcoin (GRS), Hegic (HEGIC), MobileCoin (MOB), Monetha (MTH), Multichain (MULTI), Navcoin (NAV), Sologenic (SOLO), Spartan Protocol (SPARTA), Symbol (XYM), and Tribe (TRIBE).\
- Users can withdraw these assets from Binance until September 1, 2024.\
- Privacy coins like Monero and MobileCoin, which use cryptographic techniques to enhance transaction anonymity, face regulatory scrutiny and potential delisting due to compliance concerns.\
- Despite regulatory challenges, privacy coins remain popular for their enhanced financial privacy features.

Bitcoin Runes Aren't Immune to Meme Coin Lull
news.summary:
- Prices of Bitcoin-based meme coins, including top Runes tokens, have fallen alongside other meme coins.\
- Runes protocol's first asset, Z•Z•Z•Z•Z•FEHU•Z•Z•Z•Z•Z, has fallen 43% over the past month.\
- DOG•GO•TO•THE•MOON and RSIC•GENESIS•RUNE have also faced pressure, with prices falling 20% and 53% respectively.\
- Discontent among Runes fans has been noted on Twitter, with some feeling put off by the ecosystem.\
- Meme coins on other networks, such as Dogwifhat and Pepecoin, have also experienced significant drops in price.\
- Despite the overall lull, BAMK•OF•NAKAMOTO•DOLLAR has rallied 157% over the past month.

news.headline: Bitcoin Meme Coin DOG Hits $336 Million Market Cap After Runes Airdrop
news.summary:
- DOG, a Bitcoin meme coin created through the Runes protocol, saw its market cap reach $336 million after an airdrop to holders of the Runestone Ordinals project.\
- DOG now has over 74,000 holders, making it the most widely held Runes token.\
- The airdrop distributed 100 billion dog-themed Runes to eligible wallets that hold Runestone Ordinal inscriptions.\
- The top five wallets hold nearly 10% of the total supply of DOG, with the largest holding 6.7 billion tokens.\
- DOG has a floor price of 5.28 sats on the secondary market and has already generated a one-day volume of 53.1 BTC, equivalent to around $3.4 million.\
- Other Runes collections with large holder counts include RSIC•GENESIS•RUNE, Z•Z•Z•Z•Z•FEHU•Z•Z•Z•Z•Z, SATOSHI•NAKAMOTO, and BITCOIN•PEPE•MATRIX.

BlackRock’s PFF ETF has $380 million worth of exposure to MSTR's preferred equities
news.summary:
- BlackRock’s iShares Preferred and Income Securities ETF (PFF) holds approximately $380 million worth of exposure to MicroStrategy's preferred equities.\
- The largest preferred equity holding in PFF related to MicroStrategy is Stretch (STRC), valued at $210 million (1.47% weighting as of January 16).\
- PFF also holds Strife (STRF) with a $97.5 million allocation (0.69% weighting) and Stride (STRD) with a $73 million allocation (0.51% weighting).\
- MSTR common stock is also held in the ETF, valued at $90 million (0.64% weighting).\
- STRC is trading slightly below its $100 par value at $99.94, while MSTR common stock is down 5% in pre-market trading.\
- Preferred equity securities like STRC offer bond-like fixed income characteristics with potential for monthly dividend payouts.

news.headline: Strategy’s STRD credit spread has tightened over past month even as bitcoin struggles
news.summary:
- The credit spread for Strategy’s STRD has tightened over the past month.\
- This tightening occurred despite Bitcoin (BTC) experiencing struggles in the market.\
- Bitcoin is currently priced at $85,598.40, showing a 3.83% increase.\
- Ethereum (ETH) is trading at $2,928.19, up 5.64%.\
- BNB is listed at $845.01, reflecting a 4.68% gain.\
- Solana (SOL) is valued at $124.51, marking a 4.70% rise.

news.headline: This Week in Crypto Games: 'Off the Grid' Token, GameStop Goes Bitcoin, SEC Clears Immutable
$$160M in stolen funds puts Sui’s decentralization to the test
news.summary:
- Sui’s largest DEX, Cetus, was exploited on May 22 for over $220 million, raising questions about decentralization and validator power.  \
- The attacker used faulty math in smart contracts, injecting near-zero assets to drain $223 million before the protocol was paused.  \
- Validators coordinated off-chain to freeze $160 million in stolen assets, avoiding on-chain governance or protocol upgrades.  \
- A proposed “allow list” recovery feature was withdrawn after community backlash; validators limited actions to censorship, not asset confiscation.  \
- The incident highlights ongoing debates about validator authority and censorship in DeFi, with critics warning of potential abuse and regulatory risks.  \
- Broader security issues were exposed, as other Sui protocols (Kriya, FlowX, Turbo Finance) also had similar vulnerabilities, underscoring the importance of rigorous code review.

Trove Markets perpetrator is Chinese crypto scammer, report
news.summary:
- Crypto sleuth "Eyeonchains" identified Jin Qing Qing from Shanghai, China, as the alleged true perpetrator behind the Trove Markets token launch failure.\
- Trove Markets failed spectacularly, shedding $19 million in value rapidly and retaining $9.4 million of the $11.4 million raised in its ICO.\
- The pseudonymous founder "Unwise," previously suspected, is now believed by Eyeonchains to be merely a "frontman puppet."\
- Eyeonchains traced ICO funds across exchanges like Binance and Bybit to a Gate deposit address linked to Jin Qing Qing via connections to the TAKER token and PoP Planet.\
- Jin Qing Qing is allegedly connected to 24 domains, including multiple crypto scam and casino sites, and reportedly received a $4 million investment from Foresight Ventures.\
- Evidence linking Jin to the scheme has been submitted to "the appropriate authorities," according to Eyeonchains.

Floki Releases No-Fee Crypto Debit Card, Boosting Token Fundamentals
news.summary:
- **Floki's New Debit Card**: Memecoin project Floki (FLOKI) launched a zero-fee debit card in 31 European countries, allowing users to spend BTC, ETH, USDC, USDT, BNB, and FLOKI.\
- **Card Costs and Fees**: Physical cards are priced at $33, while virtual cards cost just over $10. A 2% top-up fee applies when transferring tokens to the spending account.\
- **Global Usability**: The card can be used globally at merchants accepting VISA or Mastercard, with zero transaction and exchange rate fees, and compatibility with over eight blockchain networks.\
- **Future Plans**: Floki plans to expand the card's availability to other countries and introduce a VIP card with higher spending limits.\
- **Availability**: The cards are available in countries including Austria, Belgium, France, Germany, Italy, Spain, and Sweden, but not in OFAC-sanctioned regions or where restricted by banks.

news.headline: AI Token Frenzy Continues
news.summary:
- The AI token craze on Solana is intensifying, driven by the rapid price increases of tokens like AI16z and VIRTUAL.\
- Traders are flocking to new AI-related tokens, hoping to capitalize on the next big surge.\
- Over the past 48 hours, mid-cap AI tokens such as SWARMS, Universal Operating System (UOS), and Alchemist (ALCH) have reached new all-time highs.\
- SWARMS has increased by 140% to a $175 million valuation, ALCH has doubled to $218 million, and UOS has surged 525% to $26 million since January 1.\
- The frenzy has also led to a spike in activity on platforms like PumpFun, with daily active users nearing all-time highs.\
- Despite the hype, some investors believe that the leading AI tokens will be those that first gained traction, regardless of their user interface or experience.

news.headline: This Week in Crypto Games: 'FIFA Rivals' Announced, 'MemeFi' Airdrop, and 'Pac-Man' NFTs
Saga becomes latest victim in DeFi hacking spree
news.summary:
- Saga paused its Saga EVM blockchain on January 21, 2026, after a hack drained nearly $7 million.\
- The exploit involved an attacker minting D tokens (Saga Dollar) using a helper contract that abused IBC mechanisms, though a private key compromise is also suggested.\
- Stolen funds, including USDC and other tokens like yUSD and yETH, were bridged to Ethereum, swapped for native ETH, and currently hold over $6 million in ETH.\
- The Saga-based stablecoin D has dropped 25% following the exploit.\
- This incident continues a recent surge in DeFi hacks, with over $30 million stolen in total this year, following attacks on Truebit, Makina, and SynapLogic.

news.headline: Protocol Village: Shardeum, L1 With 'Dynamic State Sharding,' Launches Stage 1 of Incentivized Testnet
news.summary:
- Shardeum, an EVM-compatible layer-1 chain, has launched Stage 1 of its incentivized testnet, Atomium, to test for bugs and vulnerabilities.\
- Allora Labs, developer of the Allora Network, closed a strategic funding round, bringing their total funding to $35 million.\
- Conduit, a crypto-native platform, raised $35 million in a Series A funding round to accelerate the launch and scaling of decentralized apps.\
- APhone, a decentralized cloud-based smartphone, announced an initiative within the Solana Mobile ecosystem.\
- The Solana Foundation introduced Solana Actions and Blinks as developer tools to integrate blockchain transactions into any platform.\
- Various other updates include funding rounds, product launches, and appointments in the crypto industry.

news.headline: Avery Ching: Aptos Led a Year of 'VC Chains'
news.summary:
- Avery Ching, the CTO of Matonee, led the development of the blockchain Aptos (APT), which is one of several next-generation chains gaining traction in 2023.\
- Other notable chains include Base, Blast, Celestia, Fuel Network, ThorChain, Sei, Shardeum, and Sui.\
- Aptos and Sui have similar origins and goals, both aiming to scale crypto networks to handle hundreds of thousands of transactions.\
- Both chains have raised significant funding from venture capitalists and are making deals with institutional players.\
- Aptos and Sui face criticisms regarding their tokenomics and initial transaction processing capabilities.\
- This year is being called the "Year of the VC Chain," with Aptos representing this trend as it has received substantial funding and support.

news.headline: Space Protocol defends token sale amid backlash over $20 million raise
news.summary:
- Space Protocol's public token sale attracted over $20 million in demand, significantly exceeding its widely cited $2.5 million target.\
- The oversubscription sparked backlash, with critics questioning the lack of a hard cap and drawing parallels to the recent contentious Trove Markets token launch.\
- The team responded by stating the $2.5 million was a soft cap, returning more than $7.3 million in excess capital while allocating 19.6% of the total supply to the public sale.\
- Funds retained will be used for leverage pools, launch liquidity, exchange listings, team expansion, and security infrastructure necessary for building leveraged prediction markets.\
- Critics remain skeptical, pointing to perceived aggressive marketing, prior involvement in GameFi projects like UFO Gaming (whose token sharply declined), and unverified partnerships.\
- Space Protocol denies misrepresentation and plans to host a public X Spaces event to directly address community concerns before the Token Generation Event (TGE).

Solana AI Agent Hackathon Tokens Soar to $500 Million Market Cap
news.summary:
- Tokens from the Solana AI Agents hackathon have reached a combined market cap of over $500 million, as reported by Yash Agarwal on January 5.\
- The surge in demand for AI Agent tokens is attributed to the success of leading projects like AI16Z and Virtuals Protocol.\
- Data from Pine Analytics indicates that the market cap of these projects peaked at $516 million on January 4, with 64 projects averaging a market cap of $7.8 million.\
- Thirteen projects have market caps exceeding $10 million, while 42 projects are above $1 million.\
- The top five tokens by market cap are SEND ($89.7M), FXN ($66.6M), BONGO ($34.7M), ASSAI ($36.7M), and NEUR ($35.1M).\
- Despite the growth, some experts criticize the trend as "hypertokenization," suggesting many projects resemble memecoins rather than offering substantial utility.

Crashed Stablecoin Iron Bank Euro (ibEUR) Lacks Fast Repeg Path
news.summary:
- Iron Bank Euro (ibEUR) stablecoin has no immediate path back to its peg after dropping 60% in value.\
- The minor stablecoin fell from $0.97 to as low as $0.39 due to imbalanced markets in its main trading pool.\
- ibEUR has recovered to $0.72 as risk-prone traders hope for a return to its Euro peg.\
- The protocol supporting ibEUR lacks the treasury liquidity needed to stabilize the asset.\
- The depeg occurred after a trader withdrew $900,000 in USDC liquidity from the Curve pool supporting ibEUR.\
- The Keep3r Network admin suggests waiting for the curve pool to automatically repeg the price.

news.headline: CoinDesk Market Index Adds Convex, Serum, 12 Other Digital Assets
DAOs are the future of Web3
news.summary:
- DAOs have gained popularity in the Web3 space, with many successful projects operating as DAOs.\
- DAOs are starting to gain traction in real-world use cases, particularly in funding scientific research.\
- Scientific research DAO projects include VitaDAO, ValleyDAO, and AthenaDAO.\
- Blockchain and DAOs offer opportunities to transform the scientific research landscape by giving scientists more autonomy and democratizing ownership of intellectual property.\
- DAOs are also being used for fundraising for charities and campaigns, as well as other creative use cases such as decentralized ownership of real estate and sports teams.\
- Analysts predict that DAOs could be the next mainstream success in blockchain innovation.

news.headline: Science has a transparency problem. Some scientists are turning to DAOs
news.summary:
- Nature Biotechnology published an editorial advocating for the use of decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs) in science.\
- The article highlights two main benefits of DAOs in research: easier funding and greater transparency.\
- DAO governance allows researchers to collectively vote on funding projects and potentially mint resulting intellectual property as NFTs.\
- DAOs like VitaDAO, ValleyDAO, and AthenaDAO are mentioned as examples focusing on life longevity, environmental sustainability, and women's health.\
- DAOs could also enable patients in medical trials to own their treatments, as seen in the case of HairDAO.\
- The integration of DAOs into scientific research is currently limited, but VitaDAO has already minted IP-NFTs and secured significant funding allocations.

news.headline: CANTO Rises From the Ashes, Spiking 250% Before Crashing Back Down
news.summary:
- CANTO's token surged 250% on Thursday amid low liquidity and buy pressure, then dropped 60% within six hours.  \
- The blockchain's TVL plummeted from over $200 million last year to just $4.6 million after a network outage caused capital exodus.  \
- The recent volatility occurred during a saturated layer-1/2 ecosystem, with many protocols competing for liquidity.  \
- Canto has not posted on X since September, despite promising a new roadmap soon.  \
- The spike was driven by impulsive market buys and underwater positions, followed by a swift sell-off.  \
- Overall, Canto's situation highlights the risks of hype-driven trading and network outages in DeFi projects.

news.headline: Aave Gauges Community Interest for Expansion to Bitcoin Layer 2 Spiderchain
news.summary:
- Aave is exploring community interest for deploying on Bitcoin layer-2 network Spiderchain, developed by Botanix Labs.\
- Spiderchain is designed to be compatible with Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM), enabling Ethereum-based applications to work with Bitcoin.\
- The Aave-Chain Initiative (ACI) has called for community feedback on the proposal, aiming to integrate Aave's $17 billion in total value locked into Bitcoin's DeFi ecosystem.\
- This move reflects a growing interest in bringing Ethereum-like functionalities to Bitcoin, as BTC recently hit an all-time high of $93,445.\
- Aave's native token (AAVE) dropped nearly 8% in the last 24 hours, likely due to a broader market pullback following Bitcoin's surge.\
- Feedback will be used to identify and mitigate security risks, with no specific timeline provided for the deployment process.

news.headline: JPMorgan’s blockchain unit expands into MENA with 8 major bank deals
news.summary:
- JPMorgan’s Kinexys blockchain platform has expanded into the MENA region, now used by 8 major banks including Qatar National Bank, Saudi National Bank, and First Abu Dhabi Bank.  \
- The banks are leveraging Kinexys to improve cross-border payments, treasury functions, and transaction efficiency amid regional settlement challenges.  \
- The platform, launched in 2019, has processed over $1.5 trillion in transactions, with daily volumes exceeding $2 billion and a tenfold increase year-over-year.  \
- Kinexys includes components like Digital Payments, Digital Assets, Liink, and Labs, with Digital Payments supporting on-chain settlement for multiple currencies.  \
- JPMorgan aims to rewire multi-currency clearing infrastructure in MENA, enabling 24/7, near real-time settlement to address liquidity and settlement issues.  \
- The platform’s integration with JPMorgan’s FX services supports on-chain settlement in USD, EUR, and GBP, with plans to expand to more currencies.

news.headline: Can crypto fully realize the open banking fintech vision?
Ion Protocol raises $4.8 million to unlock native yield on rollups and appchains
news.summary:
- Ion Protocol secures $4.8M in funding from investors including Gumi Capital Cryptos, Robot Ventures, and BanklessVC, raising its total funding to $7M.\
- The new capital will support the development of Nucleus, a native yield primitive aimed at solving monetization issues for rollups and appchains.\
- Nucleus enables networks to offer native yield for ETH, BTC, and USD-backed assets, incentivizing deposits and generating returns on a variety of assets.\
- The platform addresses the opportunity cost of forgoing staking yields on Ethereum mainnet by providing default yield across 20+ rollups and appchains.\
- Yield is sourced from securing infrastructure products like bridges and oracle networks, with revenue passed to networks, apps, and users.\
- Nucleus reallocates unused borrower liquidity to support sophisticated counterparties, facilitating seamless liquidity movement across chains.

news.headline: First Mover Americas: Bitcoin Takes Bull Breather
news.summary:
- Bitcoin's price saw a slight pullback after surpassing $66,000 for the first time in three weeks, trading between $65,200 and $65,800, up 0.9% in the last 24 hours.\
- Bitcoin ETFs experienced significant inflows of $556 million on Monday, the highest in over four months, with Fidelity's FBTC leading at $239 million.\
- Traders on Polymarket are betting with a 64% probability that Bitcoin will reach a new all-time high in 2024, up nine percentage points from last week.\
- SingularityDAO plans to merge with Cogito Finance and SelfKey to form Singularity Finance, focusing on tokenizing the AI economy and offering AI-powered financial tools.\
- Google Search trends for "Bitcoin" have hit a yearly low, reminiscent of early 2024 when BTC surged from $41,000 to nearly $71,500.\
- Vice President Kamala Harris did not address cryptocurrency in her latest campaign speech, leaving the market speculating on future regulatory frameworks.

news.headline: SingularityDAO Plans to Merge With Cogito Finance, SelfKey to Form AI-Focused Layer-2
news.summary:
- SingularityDAO, Cogito Finance, and SelfKey are merging to form Singularity Finance (SFI), a project focused on tokenizing the AI economy.\
- The new entity will offer a layer-2 network for tokenizing assets like GPUs and provide AI-powered financial tools.\
- SelfKey's token KEY will become SFI, while SingularityDAO's SDAO and Cogito's CGV will merge into SFI at ratios of 1:80.353 and 1:10.89, respectively, subject to stakeholder discussions.\
- This merger follows a similar consolidation by SingularityNET in June with Fetch.ai and Ocean Protocol to create the Artificial Superintelligence Alliance token (ASI).

news.headline: Biological Age vs. Chronological Age: Redefining Age in the Digital Era
news.summary:
- AI-driven tools are enabling people to measure, track, and potentially reverse their biological age, shifting focus from just lifespan to health quality.  \
- Biological age reflects how well or poorly your body functions relative to your chronological age, influencing risks for age-related diseases.  \
- Experts favor DNA methylation-based epigenetic clocks as the most reliable biomarkers for estimating biological age.  \
- Several apps, like Rejuve AI and InsideTracker, use health data and AI to monitor and aim to reduce biological age, but consumers should be cautious of unverified claims.  \
- The anti-aging industry is projected to reach $140 billion by 2034, driven by increasing awareness and technological advances in longevity science.  \
- Ethical considerations around data privacy and misuse are critical as AI and health data become more integrated into longevity research.

news.headline: NFT marketplace Magic Eden acquires Slingshot trading app
news.summary:
- Magic Eden, an NFT marketplace, is acquiring the Slingshot trading app, with the deal expected to close soon, though financial details remain undisclosed.\
- Magic Eden CEO Jack Lu emphasizes the long-term potential of crypto apps, highlighting Slingshot's fast trading and cross-chain capabilities without the need for bridging tokens.\
- Future plans for Slingshot include adding social features and support for various cryptocurrencies like HyperLiquid, XRP, Cardano, Sui, TON, and Chainlink.\
- Slingshot, a decentralized, mobile-first app launched in February 2023, allows users to connect payment methods like Apple Pay and PayPal for crypto purchases, maintaining user custody of assets.\
- The acquisition reflects Magic Eden's strategy to diversify beyond NFTs, as NFT trading volumes have stagnated, similar to OpenSea's expansion into fungible tokens.\
- Despite a down market, Lu remains optimistic, viewing the current quiet period as an opportunity to focus on building and deepening their strategic vision.

news.headline: Filecoin Foundation Partners With Theoriq To Build Smart AI Agents
Nuklai and Filecoin Team Up To Decentralize Data For Training AI Models
news.summary:
- Filecoin has partnered with Nuklai to create a decentralized database for AI and machine learning.\
- Nuklai will store data on the Filecoin network using its Lighthouse perpetual storage service.\
- The database aims to provide a data ontology solution to train AI models efficiently.\
- The partnership aims to challenge the dominance of centralized cloud providers in the data storage sector.\
- The price of Filecoin (FIL) has dropped 13.5% in the past week.\
- AI tokens have experienced significant volatility, with a 37.5% crash in market capitalization.

news.headline: SoSoValue hits $200 million valuation with $15 million Series A round
New Binance CEO Yi He already had her phone hacked
news.summary:
- Yi He, who became Binance co-CEO last week, had her WeChat account hacked this week, leading to a memecoin pump-and-dump scheme.\
- The hacker allegedly spent only a few thousand dollars to accumulate a position in the token, Mubarakah, before selling when its market capitalization peaked near $5 million.\
- Binance founder Changpeng Zhao (CZ) publicly apologized for the incident, blaming the security weakness of Web 2 social media platforms like WeChat.\
- Neither CZ nor Yi He had ever endorsed the memecoin, which is named after a variation of one of CZ's tokens.\
- Yi He tweeted about the hack without apologizing, stating her WeChat was compromised because the associated phone number had been seized and the account could not be recovered.\
- Unlucky traders lost 45% of their investment from the token's peak by the time Yi He issued her clarification.

news.headline: The Solana ecosystem has a lot to be thankful for this year
State of Solana
Plans for Luxury 'Crypto Tower' in Dubai Unveiled
news.summary:
- Plans for a 17-storey "Crypto Tower" in Dubai have been unveiled, featuring luxury amenities like a gold bullion shop, exotic car dealership, and a premium networking club.\
- The development, led by DMCC and REIT Development, will offer 150,000 square feet of space for blockchain, Web3, and DeFi companies, including incubators and venture capital firms.\
- Blockchain technology will be integral to the building's management, with smart contracts for tenancies and on-chain voting for communal decisions.\
- The tower aims to be a hub for the crypto community, hosting conferences with 10,000 square feet for indoor events and 3,500 square feet for outdoor gatherings.\
- A partnership with Chatoshi, a Solana-based Web3 browser, will incorporate AI to enhance user experiences and drive innovation.\
- Construction is expected to be completed by Q1 2027, with potential revenue from naming rights for various parts of the building.

news.headline: Xsolla ZK Uses Zero-Knowledge Technology to Create ‘Digital Backpack’ for Game Devs
news.summary:
- Xsolla has launched the Xsolla ZK chain on Ethereum’s Sepolia testnet, introducing a "digital backpack" for managing on-chain virtual items in games.\
- The digital backpack allows developers, item creators, and gaming infrastructure providers to store and manage in-game items using zero-knowledge technology.\
- Xsolla ZK is part of the Elastic Chain, utilizing Ethereum layer-2 Zksync and zk-rollups for scalable solutions, enhancing the Xsolla ecosystem used by over 1,000 developers across 2,500 games.\
- Rich Kim from Matter Labs emphasized the need for proven infrastructure to support mass usage games, highlighting Xsolla's experience with major gaming firms like Valve and Take-Two.\
- The initiative aims to bridge gaps for users and builders, offering easy-to-use in-game features and payment options, facilitating the transition to Web3 gaming.

news.headline: Solana Meme Coins Slump as Investors Rotate into ETH \u0026 ‘Quality-Focused’ Altcoins
news.summary:
- Solana meme coins dropped over 8% today, with major tokens like Chill House, Fartcoin, and MLG falling double digits; BONK declined 6.5% despite a $115M acquisition plan.  \
- The entire meme coin sector is down 4.1%, underperforming the broader crypto market, which is trading flat.  \
- Ethereum surged 47.5% this month, driven by a spike in institutional interest, with $10.87B flowing into ETH ETFs and over $14B in public treasury acquisitions.  \
- Institutional investors now hold nearly 8% of ETH’s supply, viewing Ethereum as a stable, decentralized backbone for on-chain capital.  \
- “Quality-focused” altcoins like Provenance Blockchain (+10.2%) and Ethena (+40.5% weekly) are outperforming meme coins amid a shift toward deeper, more liquid markets.  \
- Solana’s daily trading volume increased to $235M over the past week, but meme coins’ share of Solana DEX trading has fallen from 73% in July to 44%, indicating waning interest within the ecosystem.

news.headline: Call of Duty-Inspired Solana Meme Coin MLG Skyrockets After FaZe Clan Boost
Karate Combat to Host Fights at Miami Stadium Named After Bonk Meme Coin
news.summary:
- Karate Combat, a crypto-fueled fighting league, will host half of its events at the new Bonk Stadium in Miami, named after the Solana meme coin Bonk.\
- The partnership involves a multi-year sponsorship deal with the Goat Shed gym, expanding its facility from 3,000 to 15,000 square feet, including a new Karate Combat Performance Institute.\

news.headline: SEC Rakes in $8.2B in Penalties for 2024, Over Half Tied to Terraform Labs
news.summary:
- The SEC announced a record $8.2 billion in penalties for fiscal year 2024, with over half ($4.5 billion) from Terraform Labs and its founder, Do Kwon, due to the Terra blockchain collapse.\
- Without the Terraform Labs settlement, the SEC’s financial remedies would have been the lowest since 2013, at $3.72 billion.\
- Despite a 26% drop in enforcement actions to 583 cases, the SEC’s financial haul increased by 65.5% compared to 2023.\
- SEC Chair Gary Gensler announced he would step down by January 20, 2025, following Donald Trump’s re-election, who promised a more crypto-friendly SEC leadership.\
- Ripple Labs’ Chief Legal Officer criticized the SEC’s record fines, suggesting they indicate poor oversight rather than success.\
- Under Gensler, the SEC targeted high-profile crypto firms, including Silvergate Bank ($90 million fine), Barnbridge DAO ($7 million fine), and NovaTech Ltd. ($650 million Ponzi scheme charges).

news.headline: How to Trade Memecoins on the XRP Ledger
news.summary:

- **Trading Steps**:\
  - **Step 1**: Set up a Ripple wallet like Xaman, available on iOS and Android.\
  - **Step 2**: Use a Telegram trading bot like First Ledger for easy transactions, or opt for a traditional DEX like Magnetic.\
  - **Step 3**: Fund your wallet with XRP, available on most centralized exchanges or via bridging from Ethereum or Solana using platforms like Rubic or RocketX.\
- **Trending Tokens**: Access trending tokens and DeFi on XRPL, with resources available on platforms like DexScreener.

news.headline: Squid secures $4m to fuel competition in one-click transfers between blockchains
news.summary:

- Squid aims to provide a seamless user experience with one-click transfers that take less than 20 seconds.\
- Other competitors in this space include bridges like Celer Network and decentralized exchanges like Rubic and Universal Swap.\
- Speed and security are crucial for DeFi middleware protocols, as hackers have targeted cross-chain bridges in the past.\
- Squid is built on top of Axelar, a cross-chain bridge protocol known for its stability during market collapses.\
- Since its launch, Squid has facilitated over $890 million in crypto bridge volume across more than 60 blockchains.

news.headline: SEC Drops OpenSea Investigation Easing Pressure on NFT Market
news.summary:

- This decision is viewed positively for the cryptocurrency industry and NFT creators, following the SEC's move to end its lawsuit against Coinbase.\
- The SEC had issued a Wells notice to OpenSea in August 2024, suggesting potential enforcement action for allegedly operating as an unregistered securities marketplace.\
- Finzer described the closure as a win for innovation in the NFT space, arguing that classifying NFTs as securities would hinder progress.\
- Chris Akhavan of Magic Eden echoed this sentiment, calling it a victory for the broader crypto community.\
- Following the announcement, the native token of NFT marketplace LooksRare, LOOKS, experienced a significant increase in active addresses, with a fivefold surge reported.

- The SEC sued Kwon and Terraform, resulting in a $4.68B judgment for defrauding investors; Kwon is now a fugitive.\
- Eligible victims (holding Terra, Luna, Anchor, or Mirror before May 2022) should read the SEC’s formal notice and avoid third-party communications to prevent scams.\
- Terraform has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy; victims need to submit proof of claim for voting on the liquidation plan, with some deadlines in August.\
- A bankruptcy lawyer can assist with the claims process, and detailed information is available on the SEC’s claims portal.

news.headline: Zora launches onchain NFT secondary markets with Uniswap
news.summary:
- Zora has launched onchain secondary markets for its NFT collectibles in collaboration with Uniswap, utilizing a new “ERC-20z” token standard.\
- The ERC-20z standard, an extension of ERC-1155, allows NFTs to be wrapped and traded like standard ERC-20 tokens, addressing liquidity issues in secondary NFT markets.\
- Initial liquidity is bootstrapped by funneling a portion of mint fees into a Uniswap pool, enabling users to purchase NFTs post-mint.\
- The first NFT on this standard, "Limitless" (ZRTK), saw 493,343 mints and raised 54.76 ETH, with daily transactions on the NFT-focused chain spiking by 181%.\
- This new standard also allows collectors to receive onchain secondary market royalties, unlike traditional offchain enforcement on platforms like OpenSea.\
- Previous experiments with new token standards, such as Bozo Finance and Ghost Labs on Solana and Pandora on Ethereum, aimed to solve similar liquidity issues in NFT trading.

- We Ghosted Media is launching a decentralized kids’ show, “Owen Nowhere,” on blockchain-based platform Lamina1, bypassing traditional TV or streaming.\
- The series will feature a crypto wallet, digital collectibles, and interactive virtual worlds where fans can influence storylines and access exclusive content.\
- The project aims to set a new standard for children’s entertainment in the Web3 space, with immersive AR/VR experiences and community-driven features.\
- Parents may have concerns about managing crypto wallets for kids, though some are already open to involving children in crypto activities.\
- Digital assets will serve as community tokens, offering loyalty rewards and creative participation rather than just financial speculation.\
- Lamina1 has raised $9 million from investors like Reid Hoffman, with the platform set to launch its Spaces virtual worlds in fall, including “Artefact” by Wētā.

news.headline: Textile, 3Box Labs Merge in Decentralized Data Tie-Up for AI Agents
Trollface Meme Creator Grants Exclusive IP Rights to Solana Token in Six-Figure Deal
news.summary:
- The TROLL meme coin team secured an exclusive, worldwide license to the Trollface IP from creator Carlos Ramirez in a six-figure deal, giving them rights to use the meme in merchandise and branding.  \
- Ramirez will earn an 11% royalty on net revenue from future TROLL merchandise, with the deal surpassing his previous earnings from Trollface licensing.  \
- The deal is seen as a pivotal moment, solidifying TROLL’s status as owning a piece of internet history and culture.  \
- Licensing agreements like this help meme coin projects protect their brand from legal issues and unauthorized use, as seen in past cases like Shark Cat and Keyboard Cat.  \
- TROLL, a Solana-based meme coin, surged to a $283.8 million market cap in August before retracing to around $159 million; it has over 44,000 holders.  \
- Ramirez has not endorsed TROLL and created his own Trollcoin, which remains small with a $47,000 market cap, but he had no plans to press copyright charges against the project.

news.headline: Apple Robot Rumors Swell—But Would One Actually Sell?
news.summary:
- Apple announced its next product event, "Glowtime," set for Sept. 9, where it will unveil the iPhone 16 and iOS updates.\
- Bloomberg's Mark Gurman reports Apple is developing a "tabletop robot" and a mobile version that can follow users, similar to Amazon's Astro robot.\
- The new hardware could debut in two to three years, aligning with other tech giants like Tesla, Amazon, and NVIDIA, who are also exploring robotics.\
- Some experts, like Philip Elmer-DeWitt of Apple 3.0, doubt the mass appeal of such products, questioning their potential market success.\
- The Apple desktop robot may feature an iPad-like screen on a pivoting base to follow user movements, but its practicality and market demand remain uncertain.\
- Apple's significant financial resources allow it to explore new markets, even if the immediate consumer interest is unclear.

news.headline: Tesla Will Pay Up to $48 an Hour to Capture Your Movements
news.summary:
- Tesla is hiring "data collection operators" to train its Optimus robots by recording their body movements using motion capture equipment.\
- The job, based in Palo Alto, CA, offers $25.25 to $48 per hour and involves wearing a motion capture suit and VR headset while performing specific movements.\
- This role supports Tesla's research into humanoid robots, which are already in use at its factories and offices, with mass production for third-party companies planned for 2026.\
- Benefits include competitive pay, medical, dental, vision plans, 401(k) with employer match, employee stock purchase plans, and various family and childcare support.\
- Candidates must meet specific physical requirements, including walking over seven hours a day, continuous coordination, and being between 5'7" and 5'11" in height.\
- Responsibilities include data collection, equipment feedback, minor debugging, and writing daily reports on observations and issues.

news.headline: Happy ₿-Day: Bitcoin’s official Unicode symbol turns 8
news.summary:
- June 20, 2025, marks the 8th anniversary of Bitcoin’s official Unicode symbol, ₿, which was added in Unicode 10.0.0 in 2017.  \
- The symbol was proposed by hardware hacker Ken Shirriff in October 2015 and became the first new currency symbol added to Unicode in 24 years.  \
- ₿ was chosen because it closely resembles Satoshi Nakamoto’s original design, and it has support from the Bitcoin community and the Bitcoin Foundation.  \
- Despite Unicode recognition, the ISO 4217 standard still does not include BTC or XBT, partly due to conflicts with Bhutan’s country code BT.  \
- The community debated using other symbols like ฿ or Ƀ before settling on ₿, which best represents a whole bitcoin.  \
- Efforts to gain ISO recognition for Bitcoin’s currency code remain ongoing.

news.headline: AI Bots to Guide Your Crypto Trades
news.summary:
- AI-assisted trading is becoming increasingly popular in the cryptocurrency market.\
- Three projects, Dither, Intent.Trade, and Taoshi Tools, are using AI to provide users with a competitive edge in trading.\
- Dither's SeerBot analyzes tokens based on historical data and assigns them an emoji-coded score.\
- Intent.Trade applies AI to onchain activities like data analysis and memecoin trading.\
- Taoshi Tools operates a specialized network that uses AI and machine learning to provide trading signals across various assets.\
- These AI-driven tools are expected to become essential for gaining a competitive edge in the evolving and cutthroat crypto trading landscape.

news.headline: Solana's Promising AI Plays
news.summary:
- Solana has attracted io.net, a decentralized network for AI and machine learning, due to its fast and efficient transaction processing.\
- Other leading AI projects on Solana include Nosana, a decentralized network for AI inference workloads, Grass, a network that gathers public web data to train AI models, Synesis One, a decentralized solution for training AI models, Dither, an AI tool for trading applications, and gmAI, an advanced AI platform for enhancing dApps on Solana.\
- These projects aim to enhance the capabilities of blockchains and AI, making them more approachable, equitable, and efficient.\
- The launch of io.net could serve as a major boost to AI projects on Solana.

news.headline: Unpacking the Balancer V2 Exploit Fallout
LIVE UPDATES: Balancer exploit drains $129M in DeFi disaster
Ethereum validator queue grows with 1.5 million ETH waiting to start staking
news.summary:
- Ethereum’s validator queue has grown to approximately 1.5 million ETH waiting to stake, amid 2.45 million ETH in the exit queue.  \
- The queue system acts as a rate limiter, processing validator entries and exits roughly every 6.4 minutes to maintain network stability.  \
- Entry wait times are extending to several days due to backlog, while exit delays are also increasing as withdrawals are processed.  \
- Native staking remains preferred by validators seeking direct control, despite higher barriers like a 32 ETH minimum and technical complexity.  \
- Growing institutional confidence in Ethereum is driving more long-term capital commitments, reinforcing Ethereum’s role as a key settlement layer.  \
- The rising validator queue indicates increased conviction in Ethereum’s future and its dominance in DeFi and stablecoin activity.

news.headline: USD.AI Bridges DeFi and AI by Turning Stablecoins Into Loans for Nvidia GPUs
news.summary:
- USD.AI has launched a platform that bridges DeFi and AI by allowing users to turn stablecoins into loans for Nvidia GPUs, enabling AI model training and deployment with crypto-backed hardware financing.  \
- The initiative aims to streamline access to GPU resources for AI developers, leveraging DeFi protocols to facilitate GPU leasing and financing.  \
- This development could potentially reduce barriers for AI startups and individual developers to access high-performance computing hardware.  \
- The move reflects growing integration between blockchain-based finance and AI infrastructure, highlighting the increasing overlap of these tech sectors.  \
- As of October 24, 2025, the broader crypto market remains volatile, with Bitcoin at $111,185.55 (+1.55%) and Ethereum at $3,959.43 (+2.05%).

news.headline: FriendTech now a ghost town as revenues fall 99.9%
news.summary:
- FriendTech, once a top app on Coinbase’s Base layer 2, has seen revenues plummet 99.9%, earning less than $60 in the past 24 hours.  \
- At its September 2023 peak, FriendTech generated over $1 million daily in fees; now, monthly fees total under $5,000.  \
- Total value locked (TVL) has declined 91% from $52 million in October 2023 to $4.5 million today.  \
- The project’s proprietary token, FRIEND, has fallen 98% from its all-time high.  \
- FriendTech’s revenue for Q2 2025 was just $9, with an extremely low EPS of $0.0000000094 per token.  \
- The platform’s initial “keys” (shares in influencers) lost nearly all value after price controls lapsed, and early investors saw little to no profit.

news.headline: Simplifying Embedded Wallets with Privy Home
news.summary:
- Embedded wallets, introduced by Privy in early 2023 and popularized by Friend.tech, simplify onboarding for crypto users by allowing wallet creation via email or social accounts.  \
- Fragmentation of multiple embedded wallets has been a challenge, making it hard to track funds and report activity.  \
- Privy launched Privy Home beta last month, a centralized dashboard to view all linked embedded wallets across various accounts like email, Google, X, SMS, and Farcaster.  \
- Users can see their total holdings, wallet details, transaction history, and manage funds across all linked wallets in one place.  \
- The platform helps users rediscover forgotten assets and better manage their onchain activities, especially for those with multiple embedded wallets.  \
- Privy Home is accessible at home.privy.io and aims to streamline embedded wallet management for the growing onchain user base.

news.headline: Funding Roundup: Stripe buys Bridge for $1.1B, Azura bags $6.9M
news.summary:
- Stripe announced the acquisition of stablecoin platform Bridge for $1.1 billion, aiming to build the "world’s best stablecoin infrastructure."\
- Stripe CEO Patrick Collison highlighted the transformative potential of stablecoins for global financial services.\
- Crypto custodian Komainu, backed by Nomura, acquired Singapore-based rival Propine; deal terms were not disclosed.\
- DeFi platform Azura raised $6.9 million in a funding round coinciding with its launch.\
- Shuttle Labs secured $6 million to develop Genius, a self-custodial crypto exchange, with investors including CMCC and Anthony Scaramucci’s SALT.\
- MoonWalk Fitness completed a $3.4 million seed round led by Hack VC, with participation from Binance Labs and others.

news.headline: Binance Labs invests in ZKsync ‘elastic chain’ Sophon in token round
Brevis Network, a ZK coprocessor, raises $7.5 million in seed token round
news.summary:
- Brevis Network raised $7.5 million in a seed funding round co-led by Polychain Capital and Binance Labs, with participation from IOSG Ventures, Nomad Capital, Bankless Ventures, Hashkey, and undisclosed angel investors.\
- The funding round, structured as a token raise, began in June and closed in September, but the post-round valuation was not disclosed.\
- Brevis uses zero-knowledge (ZK) technology to perform off-chain computations, aiming to improve blockchain scalability by reducing workload, enhancing speed, and cutting costs.\
- The ZK coprocessor is in the beta mainnet stage, with partners like Kwenta, JoJo Exchange, and Trusta already utilizing it, and other DeFi protocols building Brevis-powered products.\
- Brevis plans to launch a decentralized prover network and is developing its "SpaZK Verifiable AI" product for future release.\
- The Singapore-based startup employs 12 people and is looking to hire senior marketing and R&D professionals.

news.headline: Notional Finance brings fixed-rate borrowing to Arbitrum
news.summary:
- Notional Finance has launched its v3 fixed-rate borrowing and lending protocol on Arbitrum, a layer-2 network.\
- The new version expands beyond simple borrowing and lending to focus on strategies that leverage yield.\
- Leveraged vaults on Arbitrum allow users to borrow against their initial capital, amplifying potential yield while minimizing liquidation risk.\
- The main use case for Notional Finance is leverage, whether for speculation or yield-generation.\
- Users must be aware of the risks associated with leveraged positions, including smart contract risk and price volatility.\
- Notional Finance chose Arbitrum due to its large total value locked and DeFi-focused community.

news.headline: The Next Generation of DeFi Derivatives and Bonds - Messari Mainnet 2022
Studio Ghibli Memecoins Grab Attention After OpenAI Releases Image Generation Feature
news.summary:
- OpenAI's new image generation feature for ChatGPT, launched on March 25, allows users to create images in various styles, including the popular Studio Ghibli aesthetic.\
- This feature has sparked a surge in Studio Ghibli-themed memecoins, with over 20 such coins launched in the past two days.\
- The most successful memecoin, "Ghiblification," reached an all-time high market cap of $29 million, currently standing at $19 million.\
- OpenAI's CEO, Sam Altman, participated in the trend by sharing a Studio Ghibli-style image on social media.\
- The rapid emergence of these memecoins highlights the influence of AI-driven creativity on the cryptocurrency market.

These memecoins are driving Solana’s record $40bn trading volume
news.summary:
- **Memecoins Surge Solana's Volume**: Solana's trading volume skyrocketed to over $40 billion in a week, driven by a new wave of memecoin trading, more than doubling its previous record of $17.5 billion.\
- **Manifold Finance Plummets**: Manifold Finance's FOLD token crashed 98% to an all-time low of $0.64, amid erratic behavior from founder Sam Bacha, highlighting the risks in the crypto market.\
- **Magic Eden's Token Airdrop**: NFT marketplace Magic Eden announced an upcoming airdrop of its ME token, with early users of its apps on Bitcoin and Solana set to receive 12.5% of the token's supply.\
- **Pump.fun's Record Revenue**: The memecoin creation platform Pump.fun saw its highest-ever daily revenue of $4.1 million, with over 47,000 new tokens launched in 24 hours.\
- **DeFi Governance Updates**: Aave DAO votes to add PAX Gold, Jupiter DAO discusses JUP token airdrop allocations, and Compound DAO votes on Gauntlet’s supply cap increase recommendations.

news.headline: Manifold token craters 98% as founder ghosts frustrated supporters
news.summary:
- **Token Crash**: Manifold Finance's FOLD token plummeted 98% from its $87 peak to 64 cents amid product disappointment and lack of updates from founder Sam Bacha.\
- **Founder Silence**: Bacha has been unresponsive, addressing concerns with memes and jokes, frustrating supporters and investors.\
- **Investor Disillusionment**: Prominent backer Jordan Fish (Cobie) expressed loss of faith, noting his investment's drastic devaluation.\
- **Product Failures**: Manifold's liquid staking product failed to compete with giants like Lido, and a key liquidity provider withdrew support.\
- **Missed Deadlines**: Bacha missed multiple self-imposed deadlines for updates on new products, further eroding trust.\
- **Past Issues**: Bacha's previous project, Block Array, also faced allegations of fraud and is now defunct, adding to concerns about his reliability.

Latest in Crypto Hiring: Algorand, Others Appoint New CEOs
The Bitcoin Halving's Degen Bets
news.summary:
- Bitcoin Halving is scheduled for April 20\
- Traders are looking for high-beta exposure to BTC before the event\
- Investments in popular Bitcoin Ordinals like Bitcoin Puppets and NodeMonkes could yield massive returns if BTC price increases post-Halving\
- Runestones, a NFT collection, has gained momentum by promising holders 3 different Runes memecoin airdrops\

news.headline: Bitcoin BRC-20 Token Prices Plunge Ahead of Halving—Are Runes to Blame?
news.summary:
- BRC-20 tokens, including ORDI, SATS, Multibit, and Ordiswap, are experiencing price drops ahead of the Bitcoin halving and the launch of the Runes protocol.\
- Bitcoin Pups is the only BRC-20 token that has seen a significant increase in price, rising over 139% in the last week.\
- ORDI is down 43.4% for the week, while SATS is down 46.6%.\
- Less popular BRC-20 tokens like Piza and Pepe are performing well, with Piza up 635.9% and Pepe up 137.5% for the week.\
- The decline in ORDI's price is attributed to its correlation with altcoins and the influence of centralized exchange OkX.\
- BRC-20-based EVM tokens, including Multibit and Ordiswap, are also down for the week.\
- Traders are preparing for the launch of the Runes protocol and are potentially moving liquidity out of Ordinals projects temporarily.

Toncoin and Notcoin Plunge Amid Telegram CEO Arrest and The Open Network Issues
news.summary:
- Telegram-linked cryptocurrencies Toncoin (TON) and Notcoin (NOT) have plunged over 23% and 18.1% respectively in the past week.\
- The drop follows the arrest of Telegram CEO Pavel Durov in France, facing 12 charges including complicity in illegal acts.\
- The Open Network, originally developed by the Durov brothers, halted block production due to an "abnormal load" and loss of consensus.\
- Other tokens on the network also saw declines: GAMEE (GMEE) down 31.5%, PunkCity (PUNK) down 21.1%, and meme coin Povel Durev (DUREV) down 8.4%.\
- The launch of the Dogs (DOGS) meme coin, which reached a $673 million market cap, contributed to the network's traffic issues.

Memecoin launchpad LetsBONK pledges 1% of revenue to buyback top tokens
news.summary:
- LetsBONK, a Solana-based memecoin launchpad, will allocate 1% of its revenue to buy back top tokens in its ecosystem, starting weekly.  \
- The platform has recently surpassed rival Pump.fun in daily volume and token launches, signaling a shift in market dominance.  \
- In the past 24 hours, LetsBONK generated around $1.5 million in fees, translating to roughly $15,000 for buybacks.  \
- Top tokens like Useless Coin (market cap ~$312M) and ANI are among the main targets for these buybacks.  \
- Total market cap of tokens launched on LetsBONK recently exceeded $1 billion.  \
- Pump.fun has introduced a revenue-sharing mechanism for its PUMP token and a community takeover feature for abandoned projects.

news.headline: Bitcoin L2 Merlin Chain has lost $1.7 billion since June
Moo Deng Solana Meme Coin Blasts Past $70M Market Cap in Two Weeks
Meme Coin Takeover Teams Face Legal Entanglement, Experts Say
Developers Compete To Bring Smart Contracts To Dogecoin Ecosystem
Polygon Stops Work on 'Edge,' Used to Build Dogechain, as Focus Turns to ZK
news.summary:
- Polygon Labs is discontinuing support for Polygon Edge, a blockchain template used by Dogechain.\
- Polygon Labs is shifting its focus towards zero-knowledge cryptography (ZK) and the new Polygon CDK framework.\
- Polygon CDK is a blueprint for building layer-2 blockchains powered by ZK, which has become a core feature in the crypto industry.\
- Several projects, including Immutable, Astar, Canto, Gnosis Pay, and Manta Network, have committed to using Polygon CDK.\
- Dogechain used Polygon Edge to build their network, but it was never officially endorsed by Dogecoin leaders.\
- It is unclear how Dogechain's operations or roadmap will be affected by Polygon's decision.

news.headline: Dogecoin Disciples Take Off as PNUT, FRED Meme Coins Skyrocket
news.summary:
- Dogecoin has seen significant gains over the past week, inspiring a surge in other meme coins.\
- PNUT, a Solana meme coin inspired by a pet squirrel named Peanut, soared 1,524% to a $1.5 billion market cap after being listed on Binance.\
- FRED, another Solana meme coin named after a pet raccoon, jumped 383% in 24 hours, reaching a $129 million market cap.\
- Other animal-themed meme coins like HARAMBE, KEYCAT, MOODENG, and NEIRO have also seen notable increases in value.\
- Two tokens inspired by Jorgie Boy, a pet monkey involved in a legal dispute, are competing with market caps of $4 million and $12 million.

news.headline: Funding Wrap: AI startups score high in 2024 thus far
news.summary:
- Crypto VCs are investing heavily in AI startups in 2024\
- AI-connected startups are leading the way in early 2024 startup funding\
- Gaming studio ArenaX Labs raised $6 million for their AI Arena game\
- CharacterX raised $2.8 million for their synthetic social network app\
- AI marketplace Ta-da raised $3.5 million for selling data to train AI models\
- Lugano, Switzerland is launching an incubation program for entrepreneurs building on DFINITY's Internet Computer network

news.headline: Why Bitcoin price’s fall below $82,000 threatens to trigger ‘another crypto apocalypse’
news.summary:
- Bitcoin dropped below $82,000 on November 17, sparking fears of a repeat of past crypto collapses like Terra and FTX.  \
- The bearish trend is driven by US political and economic uncertainty, including a rising likelihood of rate cuts and global geopolitical tensions.  \
- Over the past 30 days, crypto has lost about 14% of its value, outpacing declines in traditional markets like the S&P 500 and Nasdaq.  \
- Several crypto firms and platforms, including DappRadar and Stream Finance, have shut down or halted operations amid market turmoil.  \
- Crypto stocks such as Circle and Gemini have fallen roughly 48%, and Bitcoin treasury firms like Strategy are down over 40% in a month.  \
- Despite the downturn, some analysts see potential for rebound if liquidity increases, with $253 million invested in crypto startups this week, totaling over $23 billion in 2025.

news.headline: DappRadar Shuts Down Operations
news.summary:
- DappRadar, an onchain analytics platform launched in 2018, announced it will shut down operations, with no immediate details on the future of its DAO or $RADAR token.  \
- The $RADAR token plummeted 36% following the announcement on Monday, according to CoinMarketCap.  \
- The analytics space has become increasingly competitive as onchain data has become more standardized and accessible, with new products emerging regularly.  \
- Growing institutional onchain activity has led many to prefer internal data analysis or partnerships with larger analytics firms like DeFiLlama or Artemis.  \
- No further updates were provided on the company's assets or strategic plans at this time.  \
- The shutdown reflects broader industry shifts toward more specialized and integrated data solutions in crypto analytics.

news.headline: Oasys Expands Crypto Gaming Presence in Japan With SBI’s NFT Market
news.summary:
- Oasys, a gaming-focused blockchain network, is partnering with SBINFT Market, an NFT marketplace run by SBI Group, to expand the crypto gaming space in Japan.\
- Oasys has garnered interest from gaming giants like Ubisoft, Bandai Namco, and Sega.\
- SBINFT Market now supports assets from Oasys' Hub-Layer and MCH Verse blockchains, allowing developers to sell NFTs via the marketplace.\
- Only NFTs from authorized artists and Oasys-affiliated providers are eligible for listing on SBINFT Market.\
- Key projects on Oasys include the upcoming game Champions Tactics: Grimoria Chronicles from Ubisoft and the OASYX NFT collection.

news.headline: Arbitrum’s DAO says its token should earn a yield — others aren’t convinced
news.summary:
- Arbitrum DAO passed a proposal to explore ARB token staking, sparking debate.\
- Critics argue that the proposed staking system lacks security contribution and is unsustainable.\
- Supporters clarify that the vote was to explore the idea, not activate staking.\
- Opponents believe the proposal provides no long-term value to the Arbitrum ecosystem.\
- PlutusDAO, the proposal's author, is open to feedback and exploring potential utility.\
- One suggestion is to source staking rewards from revenue within the Arbitrum ecosystem.

news.headline: Arthur Hayes turns up the ‘risk dial’ on these seven DeSci tokens
news.summary:
- Arthur Hayes's fund, Maelstrom, is heavily investing in Decentralised Science (DeSci) tokens, aiming to address inefficiencies in traditional scientific research.\
- Hayes encourages high-risk trading, focusing on DeSci, a sector valued at approximately $2.2 billion, which he believes is overlooked by many crypto investors.\
- DeSci tokens target issues like funding, access to longevity investments, and intellectual property concerns, with Maelstrom investing in seven tokens, including BIO, PSY, and CRYO.\
- The DeSci sector's mindshare increased by 2,600% in 2024, surpassing even AI and memecoins, according to research firm Kaito.\
- Hayes maintains a $200,000 price target for Bitcoin and sees DeSci tokens as an asymmetrical bet with significant upside potential due to their limited number.\
- Maelstrom's investment strategy includes tapping into platforms for funding, governance, and early product commercialization through these tokens.

news.headline: Arthur Hayes tells risk-takers to go ‘degen’ mode, predicts crypto peak in March
Elon Musk's X Reveals First Payments Details—But No Dogecoin (Yet)
news.summary:
- Elon Musk's social media platform X, formerly known as Twitter, is set to launch a payments service called X Money later this year, in partnership with Visa.\
- X Money will offer features like instant funding to an "X Wallet" via Visa Direct, peer-to-peer payments, and the ability to transfer funds to a bank account, but it does not currently include cryptocurrency options.\
- Despite past speculation about Dogecoin being integrated into X's payment system, there has been no confirmation or mention of Dogecoin in the recent announcements.\
- X Payments LLC has secured money transmitter licenses from 40 states and Washington D.C., positioning the platform to expand its financial services.\
- Dogecoin's price has been influenced by Musk's ventures and his relationship with President Donald Trump, though recent price spikes have been short-lived.\
- Visa Direct has previously partnered with crypto firms, such as Coinbase, to facilitate crypto purchases and cash-outs using Visa debit cards.

news.headline: Crypto Exchange bitFlyer Aligns Itself With FATF 'Travel Rule' With New Restrictions
Cosmos gets native EVM framework as it open-sources Evmos
news.summary:
- Cosmos’ Interchain Foundation has open-sourced Evmos, now rebranded as “Cosmos EVM,” integrating it into the official Interchain software stack, including the Cosmos SDK.\
- This move standardizes the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) within the Cosmos ecosystem, enhancing compatibility and interoperability with over 200 appchains.\
- Cosmos EVM supports full EVM compatibility, including JSON-RPC and Ethereum wallet compatibility, facilitating native ERC-20 token integration.\
- The integration bolsters cross-chain interoperability, particularly through the Inter-Blockchain Communication (IBC) protocol, linking the broader EVM ecosystem with Cosmos.\
- Evmos co-founder Federico Kunze Küllmer will transition from a core contributor role to an advisory position, while his firm Altiplanic will cease contributions to the project.\
- Originally conceptualized as Ethermint in 2016, Evmos launched on mainnet in 2022, raising $27 million in a token sale led by Polychain Capital.

news.headline: Blockchain Infra Provider Lava Network Unveils “Industry First” Incentive Structure
Genesis: How to Use Virtuals' New Token Launcher
news.summary:
- Virtuals launched Genesis, a new fair, transparent, and permissionless token launch mechanism using a "proof of contribution" points system, rewarding genuine ecosystem contributors.  \
- The 24-hour presale allows participants to pledge Virgen Points and $VIRTUAL to earn up to 0.5% of total supply, with allocations capped to prevent dominance.  \
- Key features include contribution-based allocations, automatic refunds if minimum goals aren't met, and developer token vesting for transparency.  \
- Participants can earn Virgen Points by staking $VIRTUAL, staking recognized tokens like $AIXCB and $SHEKEL, or creating content for Virtuals.  \
- Virgen Points can be boosted by holding Genesis tokens, but profit-taking triggers cooldowns; points expire after 30 days, encouraging strategic participation.  \
- Genesis exemplifies innovative token launch design amid rising experimentation in launchpads across blockchains like Solana.

news.headline: Trump's Media Group Moves to Launch Bitcoin ETF
news.summary:
- Trump Media and Technology Group (TMTG) is set to launch a series of ETFs under the brand Truth.Fi, including a Bitcoin-focused ETF called Truth.Fi Bitcoin Plus ETF.\
- Other ETFs in the lineup include the Truth.Fi Made in America ETF, focusing on U.S. manufacturing, and the Truth.Fi Energy Independence ETF, targeting American energy sectors.\
- The ETFs will be custodied by Charles Schwab, with Yorkville Advisors acting as the investment adviser.\
- Despite likely being one of the smaller Bitcoin-related ETFs, this move underscores Trump's ambition to enter the crypto market.\
- The initiative marks a significant intersection of politics and cryptocurrency, potentially influencing mainstream adoption but also raising concerns about conflicts of interest and brand dilution.

news.headline: Trump Media Registers Trademarks for TruthFi Investment Vehicles, Including Bitcoin Plus ETF and SMA on February 6, 2025
news.summary:
- Trump Media has registered trademarks for a new line of investment vehicles under the brand "TruthFi."\
- The filings include products such as the "Truth.Fi Bitcoin Plus ETF" and "Truth.Fi Bitcoin Plus SMA."\
- This move indicates Trump Media's potential entry into the cryptocurrency exchange-traded fund (ETF) market.\
- The trademarks were officially registered on February 6, 2025.\
- The initiative suggests Trump Media is positioning itself within the asset management industry, focusing on both traditional and cryptocurrency-related investment products.

news.headline: Meme and AI token indices plummet 50% from December peaks while US and DeFi indices show resilience
news.summary:
- The GMMEME and GMAI indices have plummeted nearly 60% from their December 2024 peaks, reflecting a significant downturn in meme and AI tokens.\
- Several crypto market indices, including GML2, GMDEPIN, and GMSOL, have returned to pre-U.S. election levels, indicating a broad market retracement.\
- In contrast, the GMUSA and GMDEFI indices have shown resilience, with declines of 29% and 38% respectively, benefiting from a pro-crypto U.S. government stance.\
- The GM30 index, representing the top 30 cryptocurrencies, is down 24% from its December peak, with BTC, XRP, and BNB maintaining stable prices.\
- The altcoin market has suffered significantly, with TOTAL2 down 28% since early December 2024, while BTC has only decreased by 6%, highlighting Bitcoin's relative strength.\
- The Block, an independent media outlet, notes that its majority investor, Foresight Ventures, has interests in the crypto space, but maintains editorial independence.

news.headline: Decentralized AI Society Launched to Fight Tech Giants Who 'Own the Regulators'
news.summary:
- Eight founding members have launched the Decentralized AI Society (DAIS) to combat the monopolization of the AI industry by tech giants.\
- Michael Casey, former CoinDesk chief content officer, is the chairman of DAIS.\
- DAIS aims to address four key issues: securing capital for decentralized AI, shaping AI policy, promoting decentralized AI education, and developing new distributed learning algorithms.\
- Founding members include CETI AI, Filecoin Foundation, Bloq, Hypercycle, Morpheus, Hemi, Odyssey, and Lumerin.\
- Casey emphasizes the need to challenge the dominance of companies like OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, and Apple, which he claims "own the regulators."\
- The announcement was made during the Token2049 blockchain conference in Singapore.

news.headline: Morpheus, Decentralized AI Project From Lumerin, Goes Live on Arbitrum Test Network
news.summary:
- Lumerin's decentralized AI project, Morpheus, has launched on Arbitrum's Sepolia test network, aiming to avoid the pitfalls of centralized AI models like censorship and monopoly control.\
- Morpheus utilizes "personal AIs" or "smart agents" that can be paid for using cryptocurrencies, enabling a decentralized Chat GPT-like interface.\
- The project leverages Lumerin's existing codebase, initially used for a decentralized Bitcoin hashpower marketplace, to build its core node software.\
- As a Web3 native technology, Morpheus allows users to buy/sell crypto, send stablecoins, access smart contracts, and use dapps and DeFi services, which centralized AI models cannot offer.\
- Lumerin, started in 2021, is an open-source protocol that uses smart contracts to manage peer-to-peer data streams.\
- CoinDesk, the media outlet reporting this, operates independently under the Bullish group, which has significant blockchain and digital asset interests.

news.headline: What’s the deal with Zora, Base, and content coins?
news.summary:
- Coinbase’s Layer 2 chain Base caused confusion by posting a “content coin” on Zora, despite claiming it didn’t launch an official token.  \
- The “BASEISFOREVERYONE” memecoin crashed 88% within hours, with some large wallets buying early before the crash.  \
- Coinbase states it didn’t sell or launch a token; instead, Zora automatically tokenizes user content, which is for artistic and cultural purposes only.  \
- Base’s founder Jesse Pollak called these “content coins,” claiming they have no financial expectations, but critics worry funds may benefit early buyers and insiders.  \
- The move has been criticized as “gaslighting,” with some viewing it as a way to sidestep memecoin regulations, while others see it as a risky experiment.  \
- Coinbase emphasizes that these tokens are not official, will never be sold by Base, and are meant to promote onchain culture and experimentation.

news.headline: Coinbase claims the token Base just launched isn’t actually a token at all
news.summary:
- Coinbase’s Base platform launched a memecoin called BASEISFOREVERYONE, which crashed 88% in two hours and 94% within seven minutes after its surprise release.  \
- Coinbase and its founder Jesse Pollak repeatedly stated there were no plans to issue a network token for Base, emphasizing it was not an official token.  \
- The newly launched memecoin was automatically tokenized content from Zora, not a proprietary or network token like gas or fee tokens.  \
- Despite the crash, Coinbase did not delete its announcement and continues to promote the project, with Pollak dismissing the incident as a “valuable lesson.”  \
- Coinbase clarified that the token isn’t an official Base token and that the project had no plans for a token, contradicting the recent launch.  \
- The incident has sparked criticism and complaints from users, highlighting the discrepancy between Coinbase’s previous promises and the recent token launch.

news.headline: Enso and Reservoir Launch OneStable Cross-Chain Stablecoin Minting Protocol
news.summary:
- Enso and Reservoir, with Stargate and LayerZero, launched OneStable, a cross-chain stablecoin minting protocol.  \
- OneStable enables “one-click omnichain minting,” allowing users to bypass manual swaps and bridging across DeFi networks.  \
- The protocol integrates Reservoir’s rUSD ecosystem, which includes yield-bearing stablecoins srUSD and wsrUSD, with a combined market cap of $192 million.  \
- Users can access cross-chain liquidity and yield from a single chain, helping to reduce liquidity fragmentation in DeFi.  \
- OneStable leverages Stargate’s messaging and liquidity rails to facilitate seamless stablecoin distribution across multiple networks.  \
- The launch aims to streamline stablecoin liquidity management and expand DeFi opportunities without traditional bridging.

news.headline: 95 - How Kevin Rose Invests in Web3
news.summary:
- Kevin Rose, a veteran of web 2.0 and founder of Digg, discusses his transition from web 2.0 to web 3.0, emphasizing the shift from investing in companies to investing in artists and creators.\
- He critiques celebrity NFTs as often lacking substance and highlights the importance of identifying genuinely innovative and creative NFT projects.\
- Rose is bullish on the metaverse, viewing it as a collection of interoperable digital environments where users can own and transport their digital assets across different platforms.\
- He believes in a multi-chain future, where different blockchains will serve specific use cases, and emphasizes the importance of decentralization for true digital ownership.\
- Rose is actively experimenting with NFTs through his project, Proof, which aims to explore various utility and community engagement aspects of NFTs, including real-world applications and dynamic, programmable NFTs.

news.summary:
- Phantom wallet has integrated support for the Move-based Layer 1 blockchain Sui, expanding its reach to 15 million non-custodial wallet users.\
- Originally a Solana-focused wallet, Phantom now supports multiple blockchains, including Bitcoin, Ethereum, Polygon, and Base.\
- The Sui integration allows users to manage SUI tokens, access Sui-native tokens, swap assets, and interact with dApps like Suilend and Bluefin.\
- Sui, developed by Mysten Labs, uses the Move programming language and has seen significant growth, with a market cap of $11 billion and over 7.5 billion transactions processed in 2024.\
- Phantom's multi-chain strategy began in 2023, with recent expansions including Bitcoin and the Base network, and plans to support the Monad blockchain.\
- Phantom recently raised $150 million in a Series C funding round, achieving a $3 billion valuation, but has no plans to launch its own token.
